summary.intent: Address a second round of Codex review findings on PR #674 for the ars-ui DropZone component, fix drag-validity callback semantics and accepted payload preservation, then get a clean second-pass Codex approval.
summary.outcome: All four original Codex threads were replied to, resolved, and a second Codex review was triggered. Three new valid-drag callback issues from the second pass were fixed, tests added, spec updated, and the commit pushed with full passing coverage (96.50% lines / 97.48% regions).

repo: DropZone distinguishes Event::AutoReset (preserves accepted form data, resets visual state to Idle) from Event::Reset (clears accepted payload) — timed terminal reset must use AutoReset.
SetProps must cancel drag state and timers when disabled/read_only turns on mid-drag.
max_files validation must count only DragItem::File entries, not mixed text+file payloads.
DragEnter from terminal states must cancel any pending ResetAfterDrop timer to avoid stale timer reactivation.
Enter/Move/Exit callbacks should only fire for valid-drag interactions; accepted payload should survive subsequent hovers until replaced by a new accepted drop or explicit reset.
Spec files exist alongside code: spec/components/utility/drop-zone.md must be kept in sync with callback semantics changes.
Project uses cargo xtask spec validate to check spec conformance.

code.finding: Transition effect emission logic; needed gating by current valid-drag state.
DragEnter handler was clearing stored files; patched to preserve accepted payload.
max_files check and drop rejection logic; needed to filter by DragItem::File only.
Terminal-state entry/ResetAfterDrop timer cancellation; DragEnter from terminal must cancel pending timer.
Spec sections on on_drop_enter/DropEnter/DropMove/DropExit/valid semantics updated to match valid-drag-only callback firing and payload preservation.

workflow: Codex review is triggered by posting @codex review as a PR comment; polling reaction state (👀 = in progress, 👍 = approved) via gh api is the standard way to track its progress.
Review threads are resolved via GitHub GraphQL resolveReviewThread mutation after replying inline.
cargo +nightly fmt --all --check must pass before push; formatter diffs on new code are common after logic patches.
cargo llvm-cov with --text output piped through rg for lines showing 0 coverage is an effective way to find uncovered branches after adding new logic.
Coverage baseline must be maintained relative to the PR baseline, not just the previous focused run, when new branches are added.

friction: Multiple iterative patch/test cycles were needed (at least 6 apply_patch rounds) to fix a chain of interdependent test failures in drop_zone/mod.rs.
nightly fmt introduced formatting diffs after logic patches, requiring a separate fmt pass before the final commit.
Coverage dipped after adding new branches, requiring inspection of uncovered lines and additional test coverage before committing.
Codex second-pass polling required several 120-second sleep cycles before new findings appeared.
open_items: Codex second-pass review verdict not yet confirmed in transcript — outcome of @codex review after the second push is still pending.

Leptos and Dioxus spec files (spec/leptos-components/utility/drop-zone.md, spec/dioxus-components/utility/drop-zone.md) were searched but it is unclear if they were updated to match the new valid-drag callback semantics alongside spec/components/utility/drop-zone.md.
